findings.description: init-dummy-guard host-extraction regex `^https?://([^/:]+).*` fails on two legitimate URL shapes: (a) NEXTAUTH_URL=http://user:pass@example.com extracts host='user' (the userinfo segment) instead of 'example.com'; (b) NEXTAUTH_URL=http://[::1]:3100 extracts host='[' instead of '[::1]'. Neither scenario causes a security regression because the extracted strings will never equal 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' so DUMMY-* values are still refused — the guard fails closed. But operators using either URL form would see confusing 'resolved host \'user\'/\'[\'' messages and the guard would block legitimate IPv6 / userinfo URLs in deployment scenarios that don't actually contain DUMMY values (because the for loop still runs). Bug surface is 'false-positive refusals on edge-case URL shapes', not 'false acceptance'.
findings.resolution: Two options. (a) Accept the limitation — document that NEXTAUTH_URL must use plain host form for the guard to recognize it; the conservative-refusal failure mode is acceptable. (b) Replace the regex with a small Python or awk script that uses a real URL parser; cost is alpine:3 needs python3 added (~5 MB). Recommend (a) — the spec's intent is 'refuse DUMMY on non-local'; conservative refusal on edge URL shapes is correct behavior. Add a comment to the guard documenting the limitation.

findings.description: traced_harness wrapper at decorators.py:179-219 calls _resolve_persona_role + _resolve_model BEFORE the try/except wrapping the awaited body. If those helpers raise (extremely unlikely given the defensive isinstance/getattr chains, but possible if self.persona has a __getattr__ that itself raises), the exception propagates to the caller WITHOUT emitting any span. This is a spec-edge violation of req observability.2 ('Telemetry MUST never crash the application') — the application doesn't crash but the harness invocation does, and observability becomes silent on the failed call. Pre-existing; not introduced in round 2.
findings.resolution: Wrap the entire wrapper body in a try/except BaseException that swallows non-exception failures and falls through to a (model='unknown', persona=None, role=None) span emission. Or accept the hypothetical risk and defer — none of the resolution helpers have known failure modes against the documented HarnessAdapter shape. Recommend defer unless we observe a real-world panic in the resolvers.

findings.description: _sum_usage_metadata at decorators.py:151-155 uses `int(entry.get('input_tokens') or 0)` to coerce missing/zero/None to 0. This silently accepts non-int values (e.g. a buggy SDK returning '123' as a string would coerce to 123 via int(); a list would raise TypeError; a None would coerce to 0 via the `or 0`). The current shape is fine for the LangChain Core 0.3+ AIMessage.usage_metadata contract (always int) but tightening to `int(v) if isinstance(v, int) else 0` would surface contract violations earlier instead of silently coercing.
findings.resolution: Change line 153-154 to `in_val = entry.get('input_tokens'); in_tok += int(in_val) if isinstance(in_val, int) else 0`. Same for output_tokens. Stylistic; doesn't change behavior under the documented contract. Defer.
